content: According to Zechariah 1:1, whose son and grandson was Zechariah?
explanation: Zechariah 1:1 — 'In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, son of Iddo...'
options: Son of Berechiah, grandson of Iddo
Son of Amos, grandson of Beeri
Son of Hilkiah, grandson of Asa
Son of Cushi, grandson of Gedaliah

content: According to Zechariah 1:3, what summons does the LORD speak?
explanation: Zechariah 1:3 — '...Return to me, says the LORD of hosts, and I will return to you, says the LORD of hosts.' A two-way relationship — repentance opens the way for divine return.
options: Return to Me — and I will return to you

content: According to Zechariah 2:5, what does God promise for Jerusalem?
explanation: Zechariah 2:5 — 'And I will be to her a wall of fire all around, declares the LORD, and I will be the glory in her midst.'
options: A wall of fire all around, and the glory in her midst

content: According to Zechariah 3:1, who stood at Joshua the high priest's right hand to accuse him?
explanation: Zechariah 3:1 — 'Then he showed me Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of the LORD, and Satan standing at his right hand to accuse him.'
options: The king of the Sammite people

content: What famous saying is given to Zerubbabel in Zechariah 4:6?
explanation: Zechariah 4:6 — '...Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.'
options: Not by might, nor by power, but by My Spirit
By the strength of your army

content: According to Zechariah 4:14, what do the two olive trees beside the lampstand represent?
explanation: Zechariah 4:14 — 'Then he said, "These are the two anointed ones who stand by the Lord of the whole earth."' Traditionally understood as Zerubbabel (king) and Joshua (priest).
options: Two anointed ones who stand by the Lord of the whole earth

content: According to Zechariah 6:12, who is called 'the Branch'?
explanation: Zechariah 6:12 — '...Behold, the man whose name is the Branch: for he shall branch out from his place, and he shall build the temple of the LORD.' A messianic prophecy of Christ.
options: The man whose name is the Branch — He shall build the temple of the LORD

content: According to Zechariah 8:23, what will the nations say as they take hold of a Jew's robe?
explanation: Zechariah 8:23 — '...In those days ten men from the nations of every tongue shall take hold of the robe of a Jew, saying, "Let us go with you, for we have heard that God is with you."'
options: Let us go with you, for God is with you
tags: Ten men taking hold of a Jew

content: According to Zechariah 9:9, on what does the king come to Jerusalem?
explanation: Zechariah 9:9 — '...behold, your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.' Fulfilled in Matthew 21:5 when Jesus enters Jerusalem.
options: A donkey, the foal of a donkey

content: According to Zechariah 9:9, how is the king coming to Zion described?
explanation: Zechariah 9:9 — '...righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey...'
options: Full of wrath with great hosts

content: According to Zechariah 11:12, what wage was paid to the shepherd?
explanation: Zechariah 11:12 — 'Then I said to them, "If it seems good to you, give me my wages; but if not, keep them." And they weighed out as my wages thirty pieces of silver.' The very amount Judas was paid to betray Jesus (Matthew 26:15).

content: According to Zechariah 12:10, on whom will the people of Judah look and mourn?
explanation: Zechariah 12:10 — 'And I will pour out on the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy, so that, when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced, they shall mourn for him...' Quoted in John 19:37.
options: The One whom they have pierced — and they shall mourn
tags: Look on Him whom they pierced

content: According to Zechariah 13:1, what will the fountain that is opened cleanse?
explanation: Zechariah 13:1 — 'On that day there shall be a fountain opened for the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, to cleanse them from sin and uncleanness.'

content: According to Zechariah 13:7, when the shepherd is struck, what happens to the sheep?
explanation: Zechariah 13:7 — '"Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me," declares the LORD of hosts. "Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered."' Jesus quotes this in Matthew 26:31.
options: The sheep choose a new shepherd

content: According to Zechariah 14:9, what will the LORD be on that day?
explanation: Zechariah 14:9 — 'And the LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.'
options: King over all the earth — the LORD will be one and His name one
A hidden judge of the nations

content: According to Zechariah 14:4, on what mountain will the LORD's feet stand on that day?
explanation: Zechariah 14:4 — 'On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives that lies before Jerusalem on the east, and the Mount of Olives shall be split in two from east to west...'
options: Mount of Olives, split from east to west

content: According to Zechariah 14:8, what will go out from Jerusalem on that day?
explanation: Zechariah 14:8 — 'On that day living waters shall flow out from Jerusalem, half of them to the eastern sea and half of them to the western sea. It shall continue in summer as in winter.'
options: Living waters flowing east and west, summer and winter
Fire of judgment consuming the nations

content: According to Zechariah 7:9-10, what does the LORD of hosts command the people?
explanation: Zechariah 7:9-10 — 'Thus says the LORD of hosts: Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy to one another, do not oppress the widow, the fatherless, the sojourner, or the poor...'
options: Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy, do not oppress the weak

content: According to Zechariah 8:16-17, how must the people of God act in society?
explanation: Zechariah 8:16-17 — '...Speak the truth to one another; render in your gates judgments that are true and make for peace; do not devise evil in your hearts against one another, and love no false oath, for all these things I hate, declares the LORD.'
options: Speak truth to one another, render judgments that are true and make for peace, do not devise evil, hate false oaths
Hoard goods to sell at high price

content: According to Zechariah 1:14, how does the LORD of hosts feel toward Jerusalem and Zion?
explanation: Zechariah 1:14 — '...Thus says the LORD of hosts: I am exceedingly jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion.'

content: According to Zechariah 2:8, what is touching God's people compared to?
explanation: Zechariah 2:8 — '...he who touches you touches the apple of his eye.' An image of intimate care — God's protection of His people.
options: Touching the apple of His eye

content: According to Zechariah 4:6, the work of Zerubbabel is not by what but by what? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zechariah 4:6 — '...Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.' Three clear parts; 'by abundant human money' is not in the verse.
options: But by the Spirit of the LORD of hosts

content: According to Zechariah 7:9-10, what does the LORD require of His people? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zechariah 7:9-10 — '...Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy to one another, do not oppress the widow, the fatherless, the sojourner, or the poor, and let none of you devise evil against another in your heart.' Many requirements; 'accumulating wealth' contradicts the spirit.
options: Show kindness and mercy to one another
Do not oppress widow, fatherless, sojourner, poor
Accumulate wealth to give to the temple

content: According to Zechariah 9:9, what marks the king coming to Zion? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zechariah 9:9 — '...your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.' Four marks; 'mounted on a majestic warhorse' contradicts the humble image.
options: Humble, mounted on a donkey colt

content: According to Zechariah 14:8-9, what will happen on the day of the LORD's coming? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zechariah 14:8-9 — 'Living waters shall flow out from Jerusalem, half of them to the eastern sea and half of them to the western sea... And the LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.' Three clear elements; 'returning to a simple peasant life' contradicts the universal reign.
options: Living waters flow from Jerusalem, summer and winter
The LORD will be king over all the earth
The LORD will be one and His name one
Everyone returns to simple peasant life
